Implemented the ARC2 task package for `9f41bd9c` in [__init__.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_9f41bd9c/__init__.py), [verifier.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_9f41bd9c/verifier.py), and [generator.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_9f41bd9c/generator.py). The rule is: copy the 5-wide gray edge motif to the opposite side, keep its top two solid rows fixed, continue the striped rows as a one-column-per-row staircase back toward the original side, and paint the contacted side of the first magenta row brown.

I used the summary hint as a starting hypothesis because it matched the official examples, so no `notes.md` was needed. The generator stays on-family by explicitly sampling square blue grids with a bottom magenta band, a flush 5-wide gray motif, variable top offset and striped depth, then mirroring a canonical left/right pair. No new shared primitive was needed.

Verification passed `3/3` official examples and `1000/1000` generated examples. I also opened [preview_01.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/9f41bd9c/previews/preview_01.png) and the outputs look visually consistent with the task family.
